use_caveats: The standard FTP protocol is not permitted to be enabled or used.  This is a HARD requirement.
FIPS Mode is required to be enabled.
Use of the SFTP Protocol must be implemented in conjunction with a GSA Approved Identity Provider an
Key based authentication must be implemented for programmatic or automated file transfers.
IAM Permissions must be implemented with least privilege to stored files and folders. Use multiple I
risks: The Standard FTP Protocol is insecure and does not provide encryption.
Several  Cipher Suites are available and could weaken security posture if used too excessively.
SFTP can be used without an Identity Provider/MFA Solution.
IAM Permission Misconfiguration can allow access to more data than necessary.
S3 and EFS Misconfiguration could expose data if not configured properly.
